lessons_learned: Bear Bull Traders PDF is a goldmine - 8 bull flag scanner strategies with exact filter values, publicly available. DTD 'secret sauce' is the integration, not the filters.
WallStreetZen validation: 'DTD doesn't offer anything over much cheaper alternatives, most notably TradingView' - confirms our build-on-TV approach.
Ross Cameron uses LightSpeed Trader as his broker, NOT DTD. DTD is research/chart UI only. Our moat is the full agent loop (scanner → decision → execution).
2026 FINRA PDT change: \ floor gone Jun 4 2026, replaced with intraday margin. Kay's €2K plan now viable without PDT wait.
User feedback: cost sensitivity is real (multiple complaints about \/mo). Our build is \/mo TV Ultimate + \ else - same range, much more depth.
DTD has no backtester. Opportunity for our v1.0 to lead on this.

drift_found.issue: Scanner filter values were placeholders (RV ≥ 5x for all scanners). Community values are 2x-3x with more granular price/float tiers. Not a doc drift, but a tuning gap that affects trading decision quality.

Sub-1-min chart strategy assumed TV Ultimate tier. Now confirmed: DTD uses 1s/10s/15s/24s charts. Same requirement for our build. Open Q1 still pending Kay.

drift_found.issue: Credentials section described Docker Secret as source of truth â€” Docker Secrets DO NOT work with standalone containers (only Swarm services). Root cause of all Telegram failures.
Docker Secret architecture documented but Docker Secrets cannot be read by standalone containers. Architecture was built on false premise.

fixed.action: Updated Telegram section: vault files on host are the actual source of truth. Docker Secrets are NOT read by standalone containers. New token 8940612948:*** confirmed working in both UAT and PROD.
Updated credentials section: Docker Secrets only work with Swarm services. Standalone containers use vault files as source of truth. New token confirmed working.

lessons_learned: Docker Secrets (docker secret create) only work with Swarm services (docker service create). UAT and PROD are standalone containers (docker run/compose) â€” they cannot read Swarm secrets via Docker socket API. read_docker_secret.py fails silently.
Vault files on host filesystem are the actual source of truth for standalone containers. Bind mount persists them across restarts.
Docker Swarm secrets cannot be read from inside standalone containers â€” architecture built on false premise.
PROD vault was ephemeral â€” vault dir missing on host meant container used writable layer (ephemeral). Created /volume1/Docker/PortainerCE/data/compose/10/vault/ to make it persistent.
UAT vault file had stale dead token â€” overwritten with new token via NAS Explorer in Portainer.
Restart picks up new vault file â€” confirmed working in both containers with getMe ok:true.

sessions.action: CRITICAL: Both @Marvless01_bot tokens were DEAD (404 = bot DELETED). PROD vault missing on NAS. read_docker_secret.py missing from PROD. docker.sock mount missing from PROD compose.

drift_found.issue: Credentials section: Docker Secret telegram_bot_tokenMarvless01bot claimed UAT token was live â€” actually BOTH UAT and PROD tokens were 404 (bot DELETED). stack.env still had hardcoded TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=8940612948 (revoked/dead).

Credentials Map: telegram_bot_tokenMarvless01bot Docker Secret contained token 8951488228:AAEQ4TomWhQ.E8s9QC86r which was 404 (bot deleted). stack.env PROD token 8940612948 also 404 (bot deleted). Both bots were GONE from Telegram.

/volume1/Docker/PortainerCE/data/compose/10/vault/ did NOT exist on host filesystem. Container was running with ephemeral vault (files in container writable layer, not from bind mount). Vault files (last modified Jul 13) would be LOST on container restart.

docker socket mount (/var/run/docker.sock) NOT present in compose. read_docker_secret.py NOT in compose volumes. The fix scripts (fix-prod-telegram.py/hermes-verify-fix-prod.sh) were created but NEVER EXECUTED.

lessons_learned: PROD vault missing on host: container running but vault dir doesn't exist on NAS = bind mount created empty dir in container's filesystem, not persistent vault. Vault files are ephemeral.
fix-prod-telegram.py and hermes-verify-fix-prod.sh were CREATED but NEVER EXECUTED. Scripts in temp/ are not deployed fixes.

lessons_learned: Telegram token was fragmented across 13+ locations â€” no single source of truth caused 401 errors with no clear fix path

When a secret appears in more than 3 locations, consolidate to a single source of truth with a distribution mechanism
compose files should never contain hardcoded secrets â€” use Docker Secrets + entrypoint distribution

lessons_learned: House-vs-gambler framing: strategy is a plug-in, system has veto. Different mental model than ours (scanner-as-strategy + thin wrapper). Their pattern is COMPLEMENTARY, not replacement.
Adopt-3 / Fork-3 / Skip-7 / Watch-4 framework: cleanly separates what we can copy verbatim, what we port and rewrite, what we ignore, and what to defer.
execution-safety 4-step pipeline (mode → risk limits → typed confirm → audit log) is the exact gap in our execution plane. Two-lock pattern (env + per-call) is industry-proven (tastytrade).
trading-loop engine (19 KB numpy + stdlib, 5 tests) fills our closed-self-learning-loop gap. Keep/revert gate + one-variable-at-a-time + reverted_locks are the value.
news-guard maps instrument → currency → high-impact events. Free Forex Factory JSON + bundled CSV fallback. Maps exactly to our guardrails plane gap.

lessons_learned: Lewis Jackson's 17-skill pack is a strong complementary upgrade: 5 modules ported in one session with 69 tests passing across 6 modules.

Author attribution is critical for forked code: each ported module has a clear header naming the upstream author (Lewis Jackson for execution/risk, Roan @RohOnChain for regime, Karpathy for autoresearch pattern).
Side-mapper helper (_normalize_side) is a small but valuable addition — upstream code uses different conventions ('buy'/'sell' vs 'long'/'short') and trader_agent.py uses 'long'/'short'.

Side-effects (timedelta_hours bug, infer_session timezone DST handling) only surface in tests. The infer_session test for midday (13:30 ET) caught a broken timezone conversion that ad-hoc testing would have missed.
Path discipline: write to E: drive first, mirror to C: drive for git. With 6 new modules this turn, the C: mirror would drift if not done carefully.

lessons_learned: Phase A is a different problem from local dev: the container needs env-var-driven configuration (port, env_tag, paper mode lock). Hardcoded values like 5050 break multi-env support.

CI workflow's branch trigger list must match the actual development branches. The existing 'dev' branch had divergent history (ops-trader migration), so pushing our pipeline-builder/* as-is wouldn't trigger builds. Widening the trigger + making the if condition more flexible was necessary.
Date-stamping image tags (:dev-YYYY-MM-DD) is cheap insurance for rollback. Same approach used for Prod (main branch).
Smoke tests must work both locally and in container. Hardcoded /app in subprocess calls breaks Windows. Use PROJECT_ROOT = '/app' if exists else cwd for portability.
The Python charmap codec issue on Windows crashes emoji in print() — PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 in the subprocess env fixes it. Container is UTF-8 by default; this is a Windows-only quirk.
